title: Battle of Kirrek (Great Hyperspace War)
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the battle during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]]|the [[Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)|battle]] during the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Unification Wars]]}} {{Battle |prev=[[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|Battle of Koros Major]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] |conc=[[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|Battle of Coruscant]] |next=[[Battle at Primus Goluud]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] |image=[[File:Battle_of_Kirrek.jpg]] |name=Battle of Kirrek |conflict=[[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] |date=[[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' |place=[[Kirrek]] |result=Victory for [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros]] forces''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |side1=[[Koros fleet]] |side2=[[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] |side3= |side4= |commanders1=*[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]]† *[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] *[[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Llaban]] |commanders2=[[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Pantheon]]'' |commanders3= |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2= |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=*1 Jedi Master *1 Padawan *Koros soldiers *[[Kirrek]] rebels *[[Ronika/Legends|Ronika]] prisoners *[[Unidentified Koros system battleship|Sienar Battleships]] |forces2=*[[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors *Several [[Sith war behemoth]]s *[[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Heavy *Ooroo |casual2=*Almost the entire Sith invasion force |casual3= |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|How can we fight against such an enemy?''"
"''How do we fight against any enemy? With the Force, to the best of our abilities.|Odan-Urr and Ooroo during the Battle of Kirrek|}} The '''Battle of Kirrek''' was a battle fought during the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] in [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]]. During [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow's]] invasion of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], part of his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] was brought to the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]], in an effort to conquer the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. With a battle for the system certainly forthcoming, [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]], ruler of the Koros system, appointed a [[Gender/Legends|man]] named [[Llaban]] to command the forces on [[Kirrek]]. Llaban had previously led an [[Army/Legends|army]] of rebels and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in opposition to Teta's efforts to [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|unify the system]] just a short time earlier. Llaban was told to re-gather his army, and along with the support of [[Koros fleet|Teta's forces]], Llaban was given the services of two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]] [[Jedi historian|scholar]] [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] and [[Celegian/Legends|Celegian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]], as well as prisoners from the nearby colony [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ronika/Legends|Ronika]]. The Sith force initially seemed unstoppable, and Kirrek's defenders were sent into a retreat while Odan-Urr's usage of [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] did not seem to have any effect. Finally, Master Ooroo formulated a plan and entered the fray. As a Celegian, Ooroo required a tank filled with [[cyanogen]] gas to exist in an [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-rich environment, his own native gas being lethal to oxygen-breathers; Ooroo shattered his tank and released the gas, which allowed the wind to carry it towards the invaders. The tactic worked to great success, drastically thinning the numbers of the Sith force. Soon afterward, the prisoners from Ronika arrived to replenish Llaban's forces, and led a charge into the heart of the Sith invaders. Although Ooroo perished due to the destruction of his cyanogen tank, Llaban and his forces were victorious in the Battle of Kirrek. ==History== ===Prelude=== {{Quote|Prepare for the fight of your lives! The battle for Kirrek has begun!|A member of Llaban's army|}} [[File:Llaban.jpg||thumb|left|200px|Llaban, commander of the Kirrek forces]] In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], two [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] explorers, [[Gav Daragon|Gav]] and [[Jori Daragon]], on the run from creditors on [[Empress Teta/Legends|Koros Major]], fled into the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] in their [[Starship/Legends|ship]] ''[[Starbreaker 12]]''.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 1]] There they discovered the long-isolated [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], which was in the midst of a power struggle between two [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]], the expansionist [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and the [[Politics/Legends|politically]] conservative [[Ludo Kressh]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]] In the middle of a [[Battle of Khar Delba|battle]] with Kressh, Sadow allowed Jori Daragon to escape back to [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in the ''Starbreaker 12'', although not before attaching a [[Homing beacon/Legends|tracking device]] to the ship's [[Hull/Legends|hull]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 5]] Daragon returned to Koros Major, where she warned [[Empress/Legends|Empress]] [[Teta/Legends|Teta]], ruler of the seven unified [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Koros system]], about the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] threat. Although the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] did not believe these forebodings, Sadow amassed a war [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] and entered Republic space not long after.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 2]] The conflict that was later termed the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]] was ignited, with Sadow launching a full-scale [[Battle of Coruscant (Great Hyperspace War)|attack]] on the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Teta soon began preparing for war, as a large part of the Sith force led by now-[[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] Gav Daragon had gathered in the Koros system. In a meeting in Teta's palace, Teta appointed [[Llaban]], a military [[Commander/Legends|commander]], to gather his former army and lead them against the Sith on [[Kirrek]]. Llaban had earlier led a ragtag [[Army/Legends|army]] of rebels and [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] in opposition to Teta's efforts to unify the Koros system. Teta was impressed with the ferocity with which Llaban's rebels fought in the [[Unification Wars (Koros)/Legends|Koros Unification Wars]] and granted his request for amnesty. Also, Teta promised to bolster Llaban's forces with prisoners from the nearby [[Prison/Legends|prison]] world of [[Ronika/Legends|Ronika]]. Additionally, two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], the [[Draethos/Legends|Draethos]] [[Jedi historian|scholar]] [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] and his [[Celegian/Legends|Celegian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Master]] [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]], arrived to fight by their side. Llaban soon returned to Kirrek and gathered his army, and began final preparations for the battle to come. ===Unintended Consequences=== {{Quote|...You may have more freedom of action than other Sith allow their slaves—but the greatest thing your kind can aspire to is competence in my service.And that is what I demand of you. Your work will create my glory. Begin your preparations. Succeed in my name. Fail me—and die.|Naga Sadow's hologram transmission to Yaru Korsin prior to his fateful mission to Phaegon III|}} [[File:Omen_wreck.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The ''Omen'', one of Naga Sadow's mining ships crash-landed on the uncharted planet of Kesh]] Prior to the assault on Kirrek, Naga Sadow assembled a large mining flotilla at [[Primus Goluud/Legends|Primus Goluud]]. Two of Sadow's mining vessels were the [[Sith dreadnaught]]s ''[[Omen]]'' and [[Harbinger (Sith dreadnaught)|''Harbinger'']], which were captained by the Human [[Yaru Korsin]] and the former [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] Jedi [[Saes Rrogon]] respectively.''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Precipice]]'' While Sadow's Sith forces were comprised predominantly of [[Sith (species)/Legends|Red Sith]] and [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]], his forces also included a substantial number of Human ship crew and miners. These Humans were the descendants of Tapani migrants from [[House Nidantha]] who had traveled into the [[Stygian Caldera]] where they were captured and enslaved by the Sith Empire.''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Pandemonium]]'' Prior to the battle, Sadow gave [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] Korsin specific orders to deliver a mining team led by Korsin's half-brother [[Devore Korsin]] to the [[Phaegon system/Legends|Phaegon system]]. Korsin and Saes were tasked with extracting and delivering [[Lignan crystal]]s to Sadow's forces on Kirrek. Lignan crystals were powerful [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|lightsaber crystals]] crafted from [[Lignan]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] which enabled the blade of a lightsaber to last longer and burn hotter than normal crystals. While Sadow had designated Yaru Korsin as the leader of the expedition, he also reminded the Human Sith in a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] transmission that he was still subject to the former's law and whim. While Sadow gave Korsin more freedom of action than most other Sith masters, he still warned Korsin that he only expected success and that failure would be punished by death. In addition, Sadow also dispatched the Sith [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermaster]] [[Ravilan Wroth]] and his retinue of Massassi warriors to escort the Sith expedition. The ''Omen'' and ''Harbinger'' left the main Sith mining flotilla which had gathered at the gas giant of Primus Goluud and traveled to [[Phaegon III]]. There, Captain Korsin ordered the [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombing]] of the [[Moon/Legends|moon's]] surface to clear the way for a team of [[Harvester droid/Legends|harvester droids]] to mine the Lignan. Despite their mission's success, the Sith mining fleet was [[Skirmish over Phaegon III|attacked]] by a Jedi [[Infiltrator (starfighter)|Infiltrator]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], piloted by Saes's former Jedi Master [[Relin Druur]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Drev Hassin]].''[[Crosscurrent]]'' While the dreadnaughts' [[Blade-class starfighter]]s fought the intruder, the two ships made preparations to jump into hyperspace. Hassin crashed his starfighter into the ''Harbinger'', causing a series of explosions which killed himself and pushed the ''Harbinger'' towards the direction of the ''Omen''. Meanwhile, Korsin's ship began to move out of the way too late, and the two dreadnaughts collided as the ''Omen'' jumped to hyperspace. The ''Omen'' was pushed just off course, and while in hyperspace encountered a massive [[Gravity well/Legends|gravity well]] that tore the ship apart. After a dangerous journey through hyperspace, the ''Omen'' crash-landed on a mountain in the remote [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] planet of [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]]. Since the ''Omen'' and its [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitter]] relay was badly damaged, it was unable to return to the [[Star/Legends|stars]] and its crew and passengers would remain stranded permanently on Kesh. The Massassi warriors eventually succumbed to an unknown pathogen in Kesh's atmosphere. Later, Captain Yaru killed his estranged half-brother Devore Korsin during a [[Duel near the Omen|duel]] near the ''Omen''; eliminating a rival to his leadership of the 332 stranded Sith crew and passengers. Meanwhile, Saes dueled his former master Druur on the ''Harbinger'', seeking to end the score between them once and for all. He succeeded in wounding Druur but failed to stop him damaging the ''Harbinger's'' [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]]. Druur's actions caused the Harbinger to accidentally [[Time travel/Legends|jump]] [[41 ABY|over five thousand]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] into the future. With the Lignan ore, Saes used the Force to keep his ship together until it emerged from the the warp. Upon learning of what had happened, Saes intended to return his cargo to [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith space]], unsure of what had happened to the Empire. During a [[Mission to the frozen moon|final duel]] against Druur, Saes was finally killed by his former Master, who was angered by the death of Drev Hassan. Shortly later, Druur, who had also succumbed to the Dark Side, was killed during a Lignan ore explosion which also destroyed the ''Harbinger'' and its crew. This effectively destroyed the last remnants of Naga Sadow's forces. ===The battle=== {{Quote|Kirrek defenders stand back. Get to shelter. Let me deal with these invaders!|Ooroo|}} [[File:Ooroo1.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Ooroo releases cyanogen gas during the Battle of Kirrek]] Back on Kirrek, Llaban based his defenses around a citadel, reinforcing his positions with trenches and sharpened stakes. Finally, the Sith fleet launched their [[Dropship/Legends|dropships]] containing the ground forces, which included large amounts of [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warriors and [[Sith war behemoth]]s. Although Llaban's forces fought valiantly against the Sith advance, the Sith force initially appeared too numerous to stop. Aided by the Sith [[Fleet/Legends|fleet's]] [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombarding]] of the planet from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], the Sith forced Llaban's armies to move steadily backwards. To turn the tide of the battle, Odan-Urr attempted to use an exotic [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force power/Legends|technique]] called [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] as he had while commanding Teta's forces [[Battle of Kirrek (Unification Wars)|on Kirrek]] during the Unification Wars. Odan-Urr hoped it would raise the spirits of Kirrek's defenders while simultaneously lowering the morale of the Sith. The battle meditation did not affect the single-minded fury of the Massassi warriors, however. With Kirrek's defenders calling for the Jedi to enter the fray, Odan-Urr believed all hope for victory was lost. With the Sith forces advancing, Master Ooroo left his position at Odan-Urr's side and went onto the battlefield. As a Celegian, Ooroo required a tank filled with [[cyanogen]] gas of his [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] to survive in an [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]]-rich [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. While oxygen was toxic to the Celegian, cyanogen gas was equally toxic to oxygen-breathers, and Ooroo planned to use this to his advantage. Now amidst the chaos of the battle, the Jedi Master cracked several apertures in his cyanogen tank, allowing the favorable winds to blow the dense gas into the heart of the Sith forces. This strategy worked to great success, as the gas caused many Massassi warriors to collapse and die, their skin itself having turned a light green due to cyanogen poisoning. Even though their chances at victory were dimming, the Sith continued to charge. Finally, more ships appeared in the Kirrek sky; although they were at first believed to carry more invaders, it was soon realized that the ships bore Empress Teta's insignia. The prisoners from Ronika had finally arrived, giving Llaban's forces new and fresh strength. With the released prisoners fighting at their side, Kirrek's defenders stopped their retreat and began to advance. The Sith warriors were forced to flee back to their ships, and the Battle of Kirrek had been won by Llaban's forces. ===Aftermath=== [[File:OorooDeath2.jpg|thumb|right|180px|Ooroo becomes one with the Force after the Battle of Kirrek]] {{Quote|Goodbye, Master…|Odan-Urr bids farewell to the dying Ooroo|}} The defeat apparent on the faces of the Sith was overshadowed by the grief of Odan-Urr at the [[Death/Legends|death]] of his Master, Ooroo. As the Celegian laid dying near the shattered remains of his cyanogen tank, Ooroo predicted that Odan-Urr would grow old and die among his precious [[Jedi artifact/Legends|scrolls and books]], as one of the most ancient Jedi ever. This prophecy later proved true, as Odan-Urr died [[3996 BBY|over a thousand years later]] in the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library]] on the planet [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]],[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 1|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 1]] the most ancient Jedi of his time.[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] The defeat of the Sith on Kirrek, along with Teta's victory at the [[Battle of Koros Major (Great Hyperspace War)|Battle of Koros Major]] and the Republic victory on Coruscant, effectively ended any Sith hope of conquering the Republic. Naga Sadow and the Sith fled back to Sith space and were finally defeated by Teta at the [[second battle of Korriban]]. Naga Sadow was forced to fake his death and eventually went into hiding on the faraway [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] Meanwhile on Kesh, Captain Yaru Korsin and the Sith survivors of the ''Omen'' managed to make contact with the native [[Keshiri/Legends|Keshiri]], a purple-skinned [[Near-Human]] species. With the help of the Keshiri geologist and dissident [[Adari Vaal]], Korsin and the stranded Sith were able to establish themselves as the new ruling elite on the continent of [[Keshtah Minor]]''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Secrets]]'' by claiming to be the [[Skyborn]], a race of gods in Keshiri religion.''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Skyborn]]'' While the Sith had plans to return to the [[Star/Legends|stars]] and rejoin the Sith, they eventually gave up due to a lack of spare parts and natural metals on Kesh. These stranded Sith eventually developed into the purely Human [[Lost Tribe of Sith]], which created a new Sith state on Kesh. By [[4985 BBY]], the Red Sith had been [[Red Sith Purge|purged]]''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Paragon]]'' from the Tribe while the last non-Human, the [[Houk/Legends|Houk]] [[Gloyd]], was killed during an [[Duel near the Kesh Sith Temple|internal power struggle]] within the Tribe.''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Savior]]'' Over the next five millennia, Yaru Korsin would be remembered as the Founder of the Tribe. The Tribe also rewrote its history by destroying or distorting all information relating to their enslavement under the Red Sith, particularly Naga Sadow. To cover up the fact that the Tribe's founder Korsin had not returned to Sadow in order to escape punishment for failure, the Tribe's leadership adopted the belief that they were the Protectors, benign gods in Keshiri mythology who had returned to Kesh. Prior to his death, Korsin himself concealed Sadow's hologram transmission, the last remaining relic of the Tribe's true history, by recording his [[Testament of Yaru Korsin|testament]] over it. In [[3000 BBY]], Sadow's secret transmission relating to the Battle of Kirrek was discovered and this sparked a violent bloodbath known as the [[Great Crisis]], which devastated much of Keshtah. In the end, the [[Caretaker]] [[Varner Hilts]] ended the crisis and reunited the Tribe after he discovered a map which confirmed the existence of a second continent known as [[Alanciar]] or "Keshtah Major". The new Grand Lord Hilts also commissioned a [[Testament of Varner Hilts|new testament]] which acknowledged the Tribe's origins as slaves to the Red Sith of the Sith Empire However, Hilts highlighted the fact that the Tribe's ancestors resisted their enslavement by learning about Sith philosophy and the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. While Hilts also acknowledged that the Tribe's arrival on Kesh had been accidental, he emphasized that the Tribe had turned the tables on their former Red Sith oppressors and rightfully exterminated them. This enabled the Sith Tribe to realize their "true destiny" as the rulers and slavers of the native Keshiri. [[2975 BBY|Twenty-five years later]], Hilts succeeded in [[Invasion of Alanciar|annexing]] Alanciar and bringing it under Sith rule. Nearly [[41 ABY|three millennia later]], the Lost Tribe rediscovered the wider galaxy after the [[Sith Meditation Sphere]] [[Ship (Sith Meditation Sphere)|''Ship'']] made contact with the [[Sith Tyro]] [[Vestara Khai]]. The Tribe then created a [[Sith armada (Lost Tribe of the Sith)|Sith armada]] and and sought to expand into the galaxy.''[[Fate of the Jedi: Omen]]'' This put them into contact and conflict with both the [[New Jedi Order]] and a powerful entity known as [[Abeloth]].''[[Fate of the Jedi: Abyss]]'' ==Behind the scenes== The Battle of Kirrek was an event that appeared in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire|''Tales of the Jedi: The Fall of the Sith Empire'']], a story arc of the [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and released in [[1997]].{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/95-291/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi---The-Fall-of-the-Sith-Empire-1-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire #1 (of 5)}}{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/95-305/Star-Wars-Tales-of-the-Jedi---The-Fall-of-the-Sith-Empire-5-of-5|text=Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5}} The lead-up to the battle was mostly featured in the arc's third issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''First Encounter'']], with the actual events of the battle occurring in the fourth issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''The Dogs of War'']]. In addition to these appearances, the battle was referenced in the opening page of the fifth issue, [[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''End of an Empire'']]. In these appearances, the battle was illustrated by [[Dario Carrasco, Jr.]] The Battle of Kirrek also served as a plot device for [[John Jackson Miller]]'s [[Star Wars: Lost Tribe of the Sith|''Lost Tribe of the Sith'']] series which focused on the story of a Human [[Lost Tribe of Sith|Sith Tribe]] that had become stranded on the planet [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]]. The series' first [[eBook]] ''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Precipice]]'', which was released on [[May 29]] [[2009]], established the Tribe's ancestors origins as slaves of Naga Sadow and linked their arrival on Kesh to the Battle of Kirrek. The seventh [[eBook]], which was released on [[July 18]] [[2011]], also recycled the Battle of Kirrek as a forgotten element of the Tribe's history. Meanwhile, [[Paul S. Kemp]]'s novel ''[[Crosscurrent]]'', which was released on [[January 26]] [[2010]], focuses on the story of Saes Rrogon, the captain of Sadow's second lost mining ship ''Harbinger'', and his estranged former Jedi Master Relin Druur. ==Appearances== *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 3]] {{1st}} *''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Precipice]]'' {{Mo}} {{1stID|Battle of Kirrek}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 4]] *[[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] {{Mo}} *''[[Lost Tribe of the Sith: Pantheon]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Crosscurrent]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Fate of the Jedi: Abyss]]'' {{Ab|Fate of the Jedi: Abyss (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of the Battle of Kirrek (Great Hyperspace War)}} *[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] {{Imo}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|see [[Odan-Urr/Legends|Odan-Urr]] and [[Ooroo/Legends|Ooroo]] entries}} *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide: Updated and Expanded]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{GreatHyperspaceWarNav}} {{Interlang |de=Zweite Schlacht von Kirrek |es=Batalla de Kirrek (Gran Guerra Hiperespacial) |fr=Bataille de Kirrek (Grande Guerre de l'Hyperespace) |pl=Bitwa o Kirrek (wielka wojna nadprzestrzenna) }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Kirrek (Great Hyperspace War), Battle of}} [[Category:Battles of Kirrek]] [[Category:Battles of the Great Hyperspace War]] [[Category:Orbital bombardments]]
